item.title: Natural
item.description: Every object on earth is made up of matter. This matter comes from the universe, and is as old as the reactions that formed it, up to a couple billion or so years ago. It’s not new here. Like it or not, humans still can’t create matter from nothing. We’ve figured out how to split it apart (nuclear fission), but still haven’t figured out how to smush it together (nuclear fusion).

item.title: Interested
item.description: This is a fascinating topic to me because of my experiences in graduate school. Now I don’t claim to be a genius, I barely know anything about most things. I mostly see flaws, and as a result, have thought about my own a lot. I see myself failing, it consumes my thoughts. So when I started falling behind my peers in school, I was initially baffled. I’ve always had top grades, and it’s always been fun for me.
